name: Turso DB API
what_it_does: Edge SQLite database built on libSQL (a SQLite fork) with per-database isolation, ultra-low latency reads at edge locations, and access via REST API, gRPC, and the libSQL client.
use_cases: Provision a dedicated SQLite database per agent or per tenant to achieve zero cross-contamination at the data layer
Execute SQL queries with sub-10ms latency from edge runtimes (Cloudflare Workers, Deno Deploy) co-located with agent logic
Embed a local SQLite replica in an agent process and sync periodically with the Turso primary for offline-capable workflows
Run lightweight relational queries using standard SQL without the overhead of a full client-server RDBMS
Manage thousands of isolated databases programmatically via the Turso Management API for multi-agent fleet deployments
not_for: Agents requiring heavy concurrent writes — SQLite's write serialization limits throughput compared to PostgreSQL
Workloads needing complex stored procedures, advanced window functions, or PostgreSQL-specific extensions
Applications requiring strict row-level security via a server-side policy engine rather than application-level access control
best_when: An agent runs at the edge or needs per-tenant database isolation with standard SQL and minimal infrastructure overhead.
avoid_when: The workload involves high-concurrency writes, large-scale analytics, or requires PostgreSQL-specific features unavailable in SQLite.

auth.notes: Each database has its own auth token (JWT) generated via the Turso CLI or Management API. Tokens can be scoped to read-only or read-write. A separate organization-level API token is used for management operations (creating/deleting databases). Tokens support expiry.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free Starter plan includes 500 databases, 9 GB total storage, 1 billion row reads/month, and 25 million row writes/month.
pricing.paid_tiers: Scaler plan at $29/month with 10,000 databases and higher limits; Pro plan with unlimited databases on request.
pricing.notes: Free tier is unusually generous for multi-tenant use cases. Row-based billing is easy to estimate for read-heavy workloads.

performance.rate_limits: No explicit QPS rate limits published; practical limits are determined by SQLite write serialization (single writer per database).

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: libSQL client errors map to SQLite error codes which are well-documented but require familiarity with SQLite internals. HTTP API errors return JSON with code and message fields. Edge case errors (e.g., replica lag) are not always clearly surfaced.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: SQLite's INSERT OR REPLACE and INSERT OR IGNORE provide idempotent write patterns. Agents should use these or check-then-write patterns; the libSQL client has no built-in idempotency key support.
known_agent_gotchas: Embedded replicas sync asynchronously; agents reading from a local replica immediately after a write to the primary may read stale data until the next sync cycle.
SQLite allows only one concurrent writer per database file; agents with high write concurrency must serialize writes or accept SQLITE_BUSY errors and implement backoff.
The Turso HTTP API uses a custom JSON protocol (hrana) that is not standard REST; agents expecting a typical CRUD REST interface will need to adapt to the batch statement format.
Database tokens do not auto-rotate; agents must implement token refresh logic using the Management API before expiry to avoid authentication failures mid-workflow.
Turso databases are region-pinned at creation; agents that later need data in a different region must migrate or create a new database — there is no live region migration.

security_score_components.security_notes: Per-database JWT tokens with expiry support are a strong model. Open-source libSQL core allows inspection of the fork. No SOC2 certification published as of evaluation date.
